name: aeskeyfind
title: aeskeyfind
summary: tool for locating AES keys in a captured memory image
description:
tool for locating AES keys in a captured memory image
This program illustrates automatic techniques for locating 128-bit and
256-bit AES keys in a captured memory image.
.
The program uses various algorithms and also performs a simple entropy
test to filter out blocks that are not keys.  It counts the number of
repeated bytes and skips blocks that have too many repeats.
.
This method works even if several bits of the key schedule have been
corrupted due to memory decay.
.
This package is useful to several activities, as forensics investigations.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aeskeyfind because tool for locating AES keys in a captured memory image.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aeskeyfind as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aeskeyfind [OPTION] MEMORY-IMAGE` as documented upstream for aeskeyfind.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aeskeyfind with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aeskeyfind --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
